citations.narrative:
The facility’s perimeter fence was unable to be evaluated in certain areas due to dense foliage and/or tree branches
covering it making it difficult to inspect its integrity in the following areas:
• Along the back portion of the shared primary outdoor enclosure housing both Agile and Tammar wallabies, spanning
approximately 100 feet in length.
• At the portion facing the driveway, for approximately 50 feet, by the male Tammar wallaby’s individual primary outdoor
enclosure.
• Along the left side of the shared outdoor primary enclosure housing Swamp, Bennett, and Agile wallabies, for about 70
feet.
The thick vegetation overgrowth climbing and leaning over large sections of the perimeter fence, as noted above, not only
makes it difficult to assess its structural integrity, but it also creates a ladder that may allow unwanted animals to easily
enter the facility. The perimeter fence must be 6 feet in height and a minimum of 3 feet in distance from the primary
enclosures so that it can function as a secondary containment system, as well as protect the animals by restricting other
animals and unauthorized persons from going through it, under it, or having contact with the animals in the facility.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “A” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by JUNE 22, 2026, or the applicant will forfeit the license fee
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
3.127(d) 
   
Facilities, outdoor. 
The facility’s perimeter fence was unable to be evaluated in certain areas due to dense foliage and/or tree branches 
covering it making it difficult to inspect its integrity in the following areas:  
• Along the back portion of the shared primary outdoor enclosure housing both Agile and Tammar wallabies, spanning 
approximately 100 feet in length.  
• At the portion facing the driveway, for approximately 50 feet, by the male Tammar wallaby’s individual primary outdoor 
enclosure.  
• Along the left side of the shared outdoor primary enclosure housing Swamp, Bennett, and Agile wallabies, for about 70 
feet.  
The thick vegetation overgrowth climbing and leaning over large sections of the perimeter fence, as noted above, not only 
makes it difficult to assess its structural integrity, but it also creates a ladder that may allow unwanted animals to easily 
enter the facility. The perimeter fence must be 6 feet in height and a minimum of 3 feet in distance from the primary 
enclosures so that it can function as a secondary containment system, as well as protect the animals by restricting other 
animals and unauthorized persons from going through it, under it, or having contact with the animals in the facility. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “A” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by JUNE 22, 2026, or the applicant will forfeit the license fee 
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
